what: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
title: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
text: melidosData helps load data MeLiDos field study. also contains helpers work REDCap dictionaries metadata. MeLiDos field study datasets contain wearable data personal light exposure eye, chest, wrist level 181 participants across 9 sites 7 countries, capturing 1080 participant days (≥80% available data). host questionnaires screening discharge time, ecological momentary assessment, diaries, logs, extensive auxiliary data available. load_data() downloads modality one many sites. flatten_data() combines multi-site lists one analysis-ready table, including shared timezone. REDCap functions clean codebooks, add labels/factors, perform QA checks.

title: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
text: can install development version melidosData GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("MeLiDosProject/melidosData")

title: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
code: library(melidosData) library(LightLogR) #for visualization library(dplyr) #for data manipulation  # one site -> data frame sleep_tum <- load_data("sleepdiaries", site = "TUM") sleep_tum |> select(Id, sleep, wake) |> head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   Id       sleep               wake                #>   <chr>    <dttm>              <dttm>              #> 1 TUM_S002 2024-06-03 01:15:00 2024-06-03 07:15:00 #> 2 TUM_S002 2024-06-02 01:50:00 2024-06-02 11:25:00 #> 3 TUM_S002 2024-06-01 02:10:00 2024-06-01 11:00:00 #> 4 TUM_S002 2024-05-31 00:00:00 2024-05-31 09:15:00 #> 5 TUM_S002 2024-05-30 01:15:00 2024-05-30 06:45:00 #> 6 TUM_S002 2024-05-29 01:15:00 2024-05-29 08:30:00  # many sites -> `melidos_data` list sleep_all <- load_data("sleepdiaries", site = c("TUM", "UCR")) sleep_all |> summary() #>     Length Class  Mode #> TUM 14     tbl_df list #> UCR 14     tbl_df list  # flatten list output for analysis sleep_all_flat <- flatten_data(sleep_all, tz = "UTC") sleep_all_flat |>    select(site, Id, sleep, wake) |>    group_by(site) |>    slice_head(n=3) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #> # Groups:   site [2] #>   site  Id       sleep               wake                #>   <chr> <chr>    <dttm>              <dttm>              #> 1 TUM   TUM_S002 2024-06-03 01:15:00 2024-06-03 07:15:00 #> 2 TUM   TUM_S002 2024-06-02 01:50:00 2024-06-02 11:25:00 #> 3 TUM   TUM_S002 2024-06-01 02:10:00 2024-06-01 11:00:00 #> 4 UCR   UCR_S001 2025-06-16 21:35:00 2025-06-17 06:36:00 #> 5 UCR   UCR_S001 2025-06-17 21:35:00 2025-06-18 06:30:00 #> 6 UCR   UCR_S001 2025-06-18 22:25:00 2025-06-19 06:40:00

title: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
text: following list modalities contains modality codes used load_data().

title: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
text: light_glasses, light_chest, light_wrist: personal light exposure datasets, captured ActLumus devices, recorded 10 second intervals eye-level, chest-level, wrist-level position. Data minimally checked import (range, explicit gaps, device malfunctions, time shifts). light_glasses_1minute, light_chest_1minute, light_wrist_1minute: , aggregated 1-minute intervals. Furthermore, days less 80% data coverage removed. makes datasets faster download, computationally easier work , also stable calculate light exposure metrics

title: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
text: health: Lifestyle health demographics: Demographics chronotype: Chronotype (MCTQ, MEQ) accectability: Light glasses acceptability ase: Sleep environment (ASE) evaluation: Evaluation study participants leba: Light exposure behavior (LEBA) vlsq8: Light sensitivity (VLSQ-8)

title: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
text: sleepdiaries: Sleepdiary lightexposurediary: Light exposure diary (mH-LEA) activity diary exercisediary: Exercise diary wellbeingdiary: Wellbeing diary (-5)

text: wearlog: Wear/Non-wear log experiencelog: Experience log

title: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
text: Note: labels volatile R. lost, e.g. data transformation, functions can reexecuted.
code: # 1) load dictionary and clean labels codebook_path <- system.file("ext", "DataDictionary_sleepdiary.csv", package = "melidosData") codebook <- utils::read.csv(codebook_path, check.names = FALSE) codebook_clean <- REDCap_codebook_prepare(codebook) codebook_clean |> names() #>  [1] "Variable / Field Name"                      #>  [2] "Form Name"                                  #>  [3] "Section Header"                             #>  [4] "Field Type"                                 #>  [5] "Field Label"                                #>  [6] "Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels"    #>  [7] "Field Note"                                 #>  [8] "Text Validation Type OR Show Slider Number" #>  [9] "Text Validation Min"                        #> [10] "Text Validation Max"                        #> [11] "Identifier?"                                #> [12] "Branching Logic (Show field only if...)"    #> [13] "Required Field?"                            #> [14] "Custom Alignment"                           #> [15] "Question Number (surveys only)"             #> [16] "Matrix Group Name"                          #> [17] "Matrix Ranking?"                            #> [18] "Field Annotation"  # 2) start with example REDCap export sleep <- REDCap_example_sleep sleep$sleepquality #original #> [1] 3 3 2 5 2 3 3 2 4  # 3) check expected column types against dictionary metadata check <- REDCap_coltype_check(codebook_clean, data = sleep) check$ok #> [1] TRUE check$details #> # A tibble: 16 × 6 #>    col              expected  present actual    type_ok issue #>    <chr>            <chr>     <lgl>   <chr>     <lgl>   <chr> #>  1 bedtime          POSIXct   TRUE    POSIXct   TRUE    ok    #>  2 sleep            POSIXct   TRUE    POSIXct   TRUE    ok    #>  3 offset           POSIXct   TRUE    POSIXct   TRUE    ok    #>  4 out_ofbed        POSIXct   TRUE    POSIXct   TRUE    ok    #>  5 sleepdelay       numeric   TRUE    numeric   TRUE    ok    #>  6 awakenings       numeric   TRUE    numeric   TRUE    ok    #>  7 awake_duration   numeric   TRUE    numeric   TRUE    ok    #>  8 sleepquality     numeric   TRUE    numeric   TRUE    ok    #>  9 daytype2         numeric   TRUE    numeric   TRUE    ok    #> 10 status           numeric   TRUE    numeric   TRUE    ok    #> 11 scheduledate     Date      TRUE    Date      TRUE    ok    #> 12 record_id        character TRUE    character TRUE    ok    #> 13 comments         character TRUE    character TRUE    ok    #> 14 uuid             character TRUE    character TRUE    ok    #> 15 supplementaldata character TRUE    character TRUE    ok    #> 16 serializedresult character TRUE    character TRUE    ok  # 4) convert factor numbers into factors sleep_factors <- REDCap_factors(sleep, codebook_clean) sleep_factors$sleepquality #factors #> [1] Fair      Fair      Poor      Very good Poor      Fair      Fair      #> [8] Poor      Good      #> Levels: Very poor Poor Fair Good Very good  # 5) attach human-readable labels from dictionary sleep_labelled <- REDCap_col_labels(sleep_factors, codebook_clean) sleep_labelled$sleepquality #factors with label #> [1] Fair      Fair      Poor      Very good Poor      Fair      Fair      #> [8] Poor      Good      #> attr(,"label") #> [1] How would you rate the quality of your sleep? #> Levels: Very poor Poor Fair Good Very good  #6) add arbitrary labels, e.g. for computed variables sleep_labelled <- sleep_labelled |> mutate(actual_sleep = sleep + sleepdelay*60) sleep_labelled$actual_sleep |> attr("label") # incorrect label (taken from sleep) #> [1] "What time did you try to go to sleep?"  sleep_labelled <- sleep_labelled |> add_labels(c(actual_sleep = "Time of sleep including sleepdelay (calculated)")) sleep_labelled$actual_sleep |> attr("label") #new label #> [1] "Time of sleep including sleepdelay (calculated)"

title: Load data from the MeLiDos field study
text: MeLiDos joint, EURAMET-funded project involving sixteen partners across Europe, aimed developing metrology standard workflow wearable light logger data optical radiation dosimeters. primary contributions towards fostering FAIR data include development common file format, robust metadata descriptors, accompanying open-source software ecosystem.  project (22NRM05 MeLiDos) received funding European Partnership Metrology, co-financed European Union’s Horizon Europe Research Innovation Programme Participating States. Views opinions expressed however author(s) necessarily reflect European Union EURAMET. Neither European Union granting authority can held responsible .

what: Evaluate a REDCap attention check column — REDCap_attention_check
title: Evaluate a REDCap attention check column — REDCap_attention_check
text: Replaces column logical pass/fail value based membership condition, sets label attribute, moves column end.

title: Evaluate a REDCap attention check column — REDCap_attention_check
code: REDCap_attention_check(   data,   check.column,   condition,   label = "Attention check successful" )

title: Evaluate a REDCap attention check column — REDCap_attention_check
text: data data frame. check.column Unquoted column evaluate. condition Vector accepted responses. label Label attach resulting logical column.

title: Evaluate a REDCap attention check column — REDCap_attention_check
text: data updated attention check column.

title: Evaluate a REDCap attention check column — REDCap_attention_check
code: dat <- data.frame(attention = c("A", "B", "A")) REDCap_attention_check(dat, attention, condition = "A") #>   attention #> 1      TRUE #> 2     FALSE #> 3      TRUE

what: Prepare a REDCap codebook for downstream processing — REDCap_codebook_prepare
title: Prepare a REDCap codebook for downstream processing — REDCap_codebook_prepare
text: Cleans HTML formatting codebook labels optionally filters codebook selected REDCap forms.

title: Prepare a REDCap codebook for downstream processing — REDCap_codebook_prepare
code: REDCap_codebook_prepare(   codebook,   strings_to_ignore = codebook_strings_to_ignore,   form.filter = NULL,   field.label = `Field Label`,   form.col = `Form Name` )

title: Prepare a REDCap codebook for downstream processing — REDCap_codebook_prepare
text: codebook REDCap data dictionary data frame. strings_to_ignore regular expression used stringr::str_remove_all() remove formatting fragments field labels. form.filter Optional character vector REDCap form names keep. field.label Unquoted column containing question labels. form.col Unquoted column containing form name (filtering)

title: Prepare a REDCap codebook for downstream processing — REDCap_codebook_prepare
text: cleaned codebook tibble/data frame.

title: Prepare a REDCap codebook for downstream processing — REDCap_codebook_prepare
code:
codebook_path <- system.file("ext", "DataDictionary_sleepdiary.csv", package = "melidosData" ) codebook <- utils::read.csv(codebook_path, check.names = FALSE) cleaned <- REDCap_codebook_prepare(codebook) cleaned$`Field Label` #> [1] "Record ID" #> [2] "What time did you get into bed?" #> [3] "What time did you try to go to sleep?" #> [4] "How long did it take you to fall asleep? Please answer in minutes" #> [5] "How many times did you wake up, not counting your final awakening?" #> [6] "In total, how long did these awakenings last? Please answer in minutes" #> [7] "What time was your final awakening? i.,e. when did you wake up today?" #> [8] "What time did you get out of bed for the day?" #> [9] "How would you rate the quality of your sleep?" #> [10] "Any comments?" #> [11] "Today is..." #> [12] "UUID" #> [13] "Start Date" #> [14] "End Date" #> [15] "Schedule Date" #> [16] "Status" #> [17] "Supplemental Data (JSON)" #> [18] "Serialized Result" #compate the original one codebook$`Field Label` #> [1] "Record ID" #> [2] "What time did you get into bed?" #> [3] "What time did you try to go to sleep?" #> [4] "

How long did it take you to fall asleep? Please answer in minutes

" #> [5] "How many times did you wake up, not counting your final awakening?" #> [6] "

In total, how long did these awakenings last? Please answer in minutes

" #> [7] "What time was your final awakening? i.,e. when did you wake up today?" #> [8] "What time did you get out of bed for the day?" #> [9] "How would you rate the quality of your sleep?" #> [10] "Any comments?" #> [11] "Today is..." #> [12] "UUID" #> [13] "Start Date" #> [14] "End Date" #> [15] "Schedule Date" #> [16] "Status" #> [17] "Supplemental Data (JSON)" #> [18] "Serialized Result"

what: Add variable labels from a REDCap codebook — REDCap_col_labels
title: Add variable labels from a REDCap codebook — REDCap_col_labels
text: Sets label attribute matching columns data.

title: Add variable labels from a REDCap codebook — REDCap_col_labels
code: REDCap_col_labels(   data,   lookup,   var_col = `Variable / Field Name`,   label_col = `Field Label`,   warn = TRUE )

title: Add variable labels from a REDCap codebook — REDCap_col_labels
text: data data frame. lookup data frame variable names labels. var_col Unquoted column lookup containing variable names. label_col Unquoted column lookup containing human-readable labels. warn Logical; warn labels provided variables present data.

title: Add variable labels from a REDCap codebook — REDCap_col_labels
text: data label attributes added.

title: Add variable labels from a REDCap codebook — REDCap_col_labels
code: dict_path <- system.file("ext", "DataDictionary_chronotype.csv",   package = "melidosData" ) dict <- utils::read.csv(dict_path, check.names = FALSE) dict <- REDCap_codebook_prepare(dict,  form.filter = "mctq") labelled <- REDCap_col_labels(REDCap_example_chronotype, dict) #> Warning: Labels provided for variables not in `data`: mctq_desctext_1, mctq_sleep_cycle_pic, mctq_desctext_2, mctq_desctext_3, mctq_desctext_4, mctq_reason, mctq_desctext_5, mctq_commute, mctq_desctext_6, mctq_outdoor, mctq_desctext_7 attr(labelled[[5]], "label") #> [1] "I work on ... days per week"

what: Check data column classes against REDCap expectations — REDCap_coltype_check
title: Check data column classes against REDCap expectations — REDCap_coltype_check
text: Uses REDCap codebook metadata infer expected classes compares classes data.

title: Check data column classes against REDCap expectations — REDCap_coltype_check
code: REDCap_coltype_check(   codebook,   indicator_POSIXct = "datetime_dmy",   indicator_date = "Date",   indicator_time = "time",   indicator_logical = "yesno",   indicator_numeric.val_col = c("number", "integer"),   indicator_numeric.type_col = c("radio", "dropdown"),   label_col = `Field Label`,   name_col = `Variable / Field Name`,   type_col = `Field Type`,   val_col = `Text Validation Type OR Show Slider Number`,   data )

title: Check data column classes against REDCap expectations — REDCap_coltype_check
text: codebook REDCap data dictionary. indicator_POSIXct Indicator val_col identifying datetime fields. indicator_date Pattern used labels identify date variables. indicator_time Indicator val_col identifying time-fields. indicator_logical Indicator type_col identifying logical fields. indicator_numeric.val_col Indicators val_col numeric fields. indicator_numeric.type_col Indicators type_col numeric fields. label_col Unquoted codebook label column. name_col Unquoted codebook variable-name column. type_col Unquoted codebook field-type column. val_col Unquoted codebook validation/type-hint column. data Data frame validate.

title: Check data column classes against REDCap expectations — REDCap_coltype_check
text: list ok, summary, per-column details.

title: Check data column classes against REDCap expectations — REDCap_coltype_check
code: library(gt) dict_path <- system.file("ext", "DataDictionary_sleepdiary.csv",   package = "melidosData" ) dict <- utils::read.csv(dict_path, check.names = FALSE)  coltype_check <- REDCap_coltype_check(dict, data = REDCap_example_sleep) coltype_check$ok #> [1] TRUE coltype_check$summary #> $missing #> character(0) #>  #> $missing_by_expected #> # A tibble: 0 × 2 #> # ℹ 2 variables: expected <chr>, cols <list> #>  #> $wrong_type #> # A tibble: 0 × 3 #> # ℹ 3 variables: col <chr>, expected_type <chr>, actual_type <chr> #>  #> $ok #>  [1] "bedtime"          "sleep" title: Example REDCap chronotype data — REDCap_example_chronotype
text: Example export containing chronotype questionnaire responses.

what: Convert REDCap choice fields to factors — REDCap_factors
title: Convert REDCap choice fields to factors — REDCap_factors
text: Applies factor levels labels using REDCap dictionary definitions radio checkbox fields (configurable via radio_value).

title: Convert REDCap choice fields to factors — REDCap_factors
code: REDCap_factors(   data,   lookup,   var_col = `Variable / Field Name`,   type_col = `Field Type`,   levels_col = `Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels`,   radio_value = c("checkbox", "radio"),   warn = TRUE )

title: Convert REDCap choice fields to factors — REDCap_factors
text: data Data frame containing REDCap records. lookup Data frame containing variable metadata. var_col Unquoted column lookup variable names. type_col Unquoted column lookup REDCap field types. levels_col Unquoted column lookup coded levels ("1, Yes | 0, " format). radio_value Character vector field types convert. warn Logical; warn variables lookup absent data.

title: Convert REDCap choice fields to factors — REDCap_factors
text: data selected columns converted factors.

title: Convert REDCap choice fields to factors — REDCap_factors
code: dict_path <- system.file("ext", "DataDictionary_chronotype.csv",   package = "melidosData" ) dict <- utils::read.csv(dict_path, check.names = FALSE) dict <- REDCap_codebook_prepare(dict, form.filter = "mctq")  chronotype_with_factors <- REDCap_factors(   data = REDCap_example_chronotype,   lookup = dict ) #> Warning: Variables provided but not in `data`: mctq_reason  chronotype_with_factors$mctq_work_travel #>  [1] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #>  [2] within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. car, bus, underground). #>  [3] within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. car, bus, underground). #>  [4] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #>  [5] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #>  [6] within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. car, bus, underground). #>  [7] within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. car, bus, underground). #>  [8] within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. car, bus, underground). #>  [9] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #> [10] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #> [11] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #> [12] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #> [13] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #> [14] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #> [15] not within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. on foot, by bike).  #> [16] within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. car, bus, underground). #> [17] within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. car, bus, underground). #> 3 Levels: within an enclosed vehicle (e.g. car, bus, underground). ... #original: REDCap_example_chronotype$mctq_work_travel #>  [1] 2 1 1 2 2 1 1 1 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 1 1

title: Add labels to selected data frame columns — add_labels

title: Add labels to selected data frame columns — add_labels
text: tibble column-level label attributes names matched.

title: Add labels to selected data frame columns — add_labels
code: dat <- data.frame(a = 1:2, b = 3:4) labelled <- add_labels(dat, c(a = "Column A")) attr(labelled$a, "label") #> [1] "Column A" attr(labelled$b, "label") #> NULL

what: Flatten multi-site MeLiDos data into one table — flatten_data
title: Flatten multi-site MeLiDos data into one table — flatten_data
text: flatten_data() combines named list returned load_data() one tibble keeps site provenance site column.

title: Flatten multi-site MeLiDos data into one table — flatten_data
code: flatten_data(melidos_data, tz = "UTC")

title: Flatten multi-site MeLiDos data into one table — flatten_data
text: melidos_data list returned load_data() multiple sites. tz Time zone enforce POSIXct columns.

title: Flatten multi-site MeLiDos data into one table — flatten_data
text: tibble rows stacked site column.

title: Flatten multi-site MeLiDos data into one table — flatten_data
text: date-time columns present (POSIXct), timezone overwritten using lubridate::force_tz().

title: Flatten multi-site MeLiDos data into one table — flatten_data
code: example_multi_site <- structure(   list(     TUM = data.frame(id = 1, bedtime = as.POSIXct("2024-01-01 22:00:00", tz = "UTC")),     UCR = data.frame(id = 2, bedtime = as.POSIXct("2024-01-02 22:30:00", tz = "UTC"))   ),   class = c("melidos_data", "list") )  flatten_data(example_multi_site, tz = "Europe/Berlin") #>   site id             bedtime #> 1  TUM  1 2024-01-01 22:00:00 #> 2  UCR  2 2024-01-02 22:30:00

what: Load MeLiDos datasets from project repositories — load_data
title: Load MeLiDos datasets from project repositories — load_data
text: load_data() main entry point package. downloads one modality one MeLiDos sites returns either single data frame (one site) named list class "melidos_data" (multiple sites).

title: Load MeLiDos datasets from project repositories — load_data
code: load_data(   modality = c("light_glasses", "light_chest", "light_wrist", "light_glasses_1minute",     "light_chest_1minute", "light_wrist_1minute", "acceptability", "ase", "chronotype",     "demographics", "evaluation", "health", "leba", "vlsq8", "currentconditions",     "exercisediary", "experiencelog", "lightexposurediary", "sleepdiaries", "wearlog",     "wellbeingdiary"),   site = c("all", "BAUA", "FUSPCEU", "IZTECH", "KNUST", "MPI", "RISE", "THUAS", "TUM",     "UCR") )

title: Load MeLiDos datasets from project repositories — load_data
text: https://github.com/MeLiDosProject

title: Load MeLiDos datasets from project repositories — load_data
text: modality Dataset load. site Site(s) load. Use "" available sites.

title: Load MeLiDos datasets from project repositories — load_data
text: data frame one site selected, melidos_data list multiple sites.

title: Load MeLiDos datasets from project repositories — load_data
text: Use flatten_data() stack multi-site results one tibble site column. See README package description sites modalities.

title: Load MeLiDos datasets from project repositories — load_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # load one questionnaire modality for all sites sleep_all <- load_data("sleepdiaries", site = "all")  # flatten to a single tibble with a site column sleep_flat <- flatten_data(sleep_all, tz = "UTC") head(sleep_flat)  # load one site only (returns a data frame) sleep_tum <- load_data("sleepdiaries", site = "TUM") } # }

title: Summarise times across midnight — nighttime_switch
text: Converts times cutoff next day applying .fun, helps summarise nighttime values spanning midnight (example, median sleep time).

title: Summarise times across midnight — nighttime_switch
code: nighttime_switch(   datetime,   .fun = stats::median,   cutoff = 12 * 60 * 60,   hms = TRUE )

title: Summarise times across midnight — nighttime_switch
text: datetime date-time vector coercible POSIXct. .fun Summary function applied date shift. cutoff Cutoff seconds since midnight. Values cutoff treated belonging next day. hms Logical; TRUE, return hms object.

title: Summarise times across midnight — nighttime_switch
text: summarised time hms (default) date-time.

title: Summarise times across midnight — nighttime_switch
